_comment: Scope: the bridge carries ONLY the 3 cabin signals between VM1 and
VM2; battery and range are NOT bridged. Nothing on VM2 consumes
battery / range today, so they are kept off the wire to reduce log
noise and clarify the bridge's purpose.
- all 3 cabin signals are BIDIRECTIONAL so the dashboard-driven
values sourced by hvac_ecu.py / seat_ecu.py on VM2 still reach
VM1, AND the EV Range Extender app on VM1 can push HVAC / Seat
setpoint changes back to VM2's Kuksa. The HVAC / Seat ECUs
subscribe to their local Kuksa for those signals and (a) emit
an 'ACT' log line and (b) forward a key/value status pair to
the host dashboard for the on-screen indicator.
VM2 dials VM1 on tcp/192.168.100.10:7448 (the new bridge listener).
The same Zenoh connection carries traffic in both directions.

signals._role: VM1 <-> VM2: cabin (bidirectional - EV-app writes flow VM1 -> VM2)
VM1 <-> VM2: cabin (bidirectional - EV-app writes flow VM1 -> VM2)
VM1 <-> VM2: cabin (bidirectional - EV-app writes flow VM1 -> VM2)
